mnemonics.title: Two IPSec Protocols
mnemonics.content:
**AH** = **A**uthentication only (**H**ash but no hiding)
**ESP** = **E**ncryption + **S**igning **P**rotection
AH: Integrity + Auth (NO encryption)
ESP: Confidentiality + Integrity + Auth
Remember: ESP does Everything; AH is only Auth

mnemonics.title: Two IPSec Modes
mnemonics.content:
**T**ransport = **T**op layer only (payload protected)
**T**unnel = **T**otal packet wrapped (entire packet in new one)
Transport: Host-to-Host, original IP header stays
Tunnel: Gateway-to-Gateway, VPN uses this

mnemonics.title: SA Triple Identifier
mnemonics.content:
**S**PI + **D**estination IP + **P**rotocol = **SDP**
Think: Every SA has an SDP card (ID card)
SPI = Security Parameter Index (unique number)
D = Where it goes; P = AH or ESP

mnemonics.title: SPD Actions
mnemonics.content:
**P**rotect **B**ypass **D**iscard = **PBD**
Think: traffic **P**oliceman at the gate
**P**rotect: Apply IPSec (secure traffic)
**B**ypass: Let it through (no IPSec needed)
**D**iscard: Drop it (forbidden traffic)

mnemonics.title: IKE Two Phases
mnemonics.content:
**Phase 1**: Build the **road** (secure IKE SA channel)
**Phase 2**: Drive the **car** (negotiate IPSec SA)
Phase 1 uses Diffie-Hellman for shared secret
Phase 2 uses Phase 1's road to safely negotiate
Phase 1 = slow setup; Phase 2 = fast negotiation

keyPoints: IPSec provides confidentiality, integrity, and authentication at the network layer (IP layer).
Two protocols: AH (auth + integrity only) and ESP (encryption + auth + integrity).
Two modes: Transport (payload only, host-to-host) and Tunnel (entire packet, VPN gateways).
Security Associations (SAs) define the parameters; identified by SPI + Dest IP + Protocol.
IKE automates SA negotiation: Phase 1 builds secure channel, Phase 2 negotiates IPSec parameters.
commonMistakes: Confusing AH and ESP: AH does NOT provide encryption/confidentiality - only integrity and authentication. ESP provides all three services.
Mixing up Transport and Tunnel modes: Transport protects only the payload (host-to-host), while Tunnel wraps the entire original packet (gateway-to-gateway VPN).
Forgetting that Security Associations are ONE-WAY: a two-way communication requires TWO SAs, one in each direction.
